summary: Tested both backend and frontend per review request. Backend: created /app/backend/tests/test_delivery_flow.py with 7 pytest cases covering delivery happy path (login -> available-orders -> accept -> my-orders -> PATCH status=delivered), negative paths (non-delivery 403, double-accept rejected, accept on non-ready order rejected), manager assignment (POST /api/delivery/assign-order with delivery_person_id) and orders/manage regression with assigned_delivery_partner. All 7 pass. Frontend: validated mobile delivery dashboard at 480x900 (login as delivery1 auto-redirects to /delivery, Available + My Orders tabs render, Accept Order works, order moves to My Orders, Mark Delivered transitions to 'Delivered Successfully'). ImageEditor verified on New Order page (Crop / Pen tabs, Cancel closes without uploading, Use This Image POSTs to /api/upload-image and primary cake preview renders) and on Manage Orders edit dialog (Edit Image modal with Crop / Pen / Cancel / Use This Image opens after picking a file).

issues: Status pill shown next to order # reads 'Ready' for an order whose actual backend status is 'ready_to_deliver'. Cosmetic only — could read 'Ready to Deliver' for clarity.

action_items: (Optional) Make DeliveryDashboard status label show 'Ready to Deliver' instead of 'Ready' to match server status.
critical_code_review_comments: delivery/accept-order does not clear/check assigned_by; relying solely on status='ready_to_deliver' for the gate. Acceptable, but worth noting for future race conditions.

ImageEditor.buildOutputBlob handles no-crop case but uses completedCrop dimensions for stroke scaling — if user only draws strokes (no crop), strokes scale correctly because xRatio/yRatio fall back to drawnW/drawnH; verified by code review.
ManageOrders edit dialog file input has no data-testid; consider adding 'edit-cake-image-input' for stable e2e selectors.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create their own TEST_ orders and walk them confirmed -> ready -> ready_to_deliver via PATCH /api/orders/{id}/status. Slot cap is set to 0 inside the fixture to avoid 409 collisions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_delivery_flow.py -v. Tests assume seed_data.py has been run (manager+kitchen users) and delivery1 user exists. Frontend UI uses data-testids: tab-available-orders, tab-my-orders, accept-order-btn-{id}, mark-delivered-btn-{id}, delivery-logout-btn, refresh-orders-btn, img-edit-crop-btn, img-edit-pen-btn, img-edit-cancel, img-edit-confirm. Manage Orders edit row uses a Radix dropdown (button[aria-haspopup='menu']) -> 'Edit Order' menu item to open the edit dialog.
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found in the iteration.
